refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain a comprehensive digital representation of the worksite environment.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR contained multiple distinct data types (terrain, obstacles, zones) which represent different functional obligations. By refining it to a high-level requirement and splitting the specific data types into atomic sub-FRs, we ensure each feature can be verified independently while remaining solution-neutral regarding the sensing technology used.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Compare the generated digital worksite model against a ground-truth survey and predefined zone coordinates to ensure spatial accuracy and feature presence.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall characterize the worksite terrain geometry.
The NGHE solution shall identify and locate physical obstacles within the worksite.
The NGHE solution shall represent defined operational work-zone boundaries.

coupling_check.notes: The DP maintains independence by acting as a state provider (source of truth) rather than a controller. It decouples perception logic from planning logic.

assumptions_risks: Assumes a standardized coordinate reference system across all site features
Risk of high latency between physical state change and digital twin update
Assumes availability of high-fidelity data for safety-critical site features
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure the DP explicitly defines the interface for 'operational policies' to be mapped onto spatial constraints within the twin.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The Next Generation Hosting Environment (NGHE) provides the centralized computational infrastructure and data fusion services required to maintain a high-fidelity digital representation of the operational environment.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingestion of real-time sensor data and state vectors from the vehicle to update the twin.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Providing environmental context and path-planning constraints to the vehicle or operator.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Triggering alerts when the digital twin detects proximity violations or environmental hazards.

module_containment_scores.notes: Ideal for high-compute data fusion; minimizes diffusion by centralizing the world model.
Onboard compute is often too limited for a full dynamic twin; results in high diffusion to external monitors.
Teleoperation uses the twin but should not own the logic for maintaining it, as the twin must persist without an active operator.
Too narrow; safety services are consumers of the twin's data rather than the generative source.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: By placing the Dynamic Environmental Digital Twin within the NGHE Solution, we centralize the heavy processing of multi-source telemetry, ensuring a single source of truth that can serve both Safety Services and Teleoperation modules via standardized interfaces.
containment_risks: Latency in the Network Link may cause the digital twin to lag behind the physical vehicle state.
High bandwidth requirements for raw telemetry ingestion into NGHE.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Dynamic environmental digital twin' directly addresses the FR's requirement for a comprehensive digital representation. It provides the logical mechanism to fulfill the acceptance criteria of generating a worksite model/map for planning and execution functions.

diffusion_risks: Network latency between the physical vehicle and the NGHE IM may cause the digital twin state to diverge from reality, potentially requiring local 'shadow' twins on the vehicle.
High bandwidth requirements for raw telemetry could force pre-processing logic onto the vehicle, diffusing the 'data fusion' responsibility.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution IM shows high containment (0.9) for the DP, centralizing the world model logic. This prevents the 'split-brain' scenario common when multiple modules (Safety, Teleop) attempt to maintain their own environmental state.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High; the presence of the 'Telemetry' (in) and 'Tasking/SafetyAlert' (out) interfaces allows for black-box testing of the digital twin's accuracy and latency against the physical worksite state.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear; the NGHE is explicitly defined as the owner of the computational infrastructure and data fusion services, aligning with the Cloud Infrastructure / Backend Engineering Team ownership.

recommended_revisions: Define a 'Local State Observer' DP on the vehicle to handle high-frequency safety checks, mitigating the latency risk identified in the IM containment analysis.
Specify the 'agreed set of site features' mentioned in the acceptance criteria within the IM scope to bound the data fusion requirements.

decision.justification: While the high-level DP and IM are conceptually sound, the 'high' safety criticality and the identified diffusion risks regarding network latency and data fusion require a more granular decomposition. The gate results specifically recommended defining a 'Local State Observer' and specifying 'site features' to ensure the digital twin remains synchronized and safe for real-time operations. Decomposing to the next level is necessary to separate high-frequency local safety logic from low-frequency global planning logic.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1.1: Maintain high-frequency local obstacle and terrain state for immediate collision avoidance.
FR1.2: Aggregate multi-source telemetry into a global persistent worksite map.
FR1.3: Synchronize local and global state representations across intermittent network conditions.
FR1.4: Classify and update dynamic site features (personnel, equipment, exclusion zones) within the model.

revise.why: The current DP 'Dynamic environmental digital twin' is too monolithic for a high-safety system; it needs to be split into local (edge) and global (cloud) design parameters to address the latency risks identified in the IM gate.

assumptions: The vehicle has sufficient onboard compute to host a local state observer.
The NGHE infrastructure supports asynchronous state updates.
open_risks: State divergence between the vehicle's local view and the NGHE global view during network outages.
Bandwidth saturation from raw telemetry streams.
evidence_needed: Definition of the minimum required feature set for the worksite model to support safety functions.
